items.headline: Major commercial project breaks ground on West San Carlos
items.summary: A $696,570 commercial/industrial development is underway at 1300 W San Carlos St, representing significant investment in San José's commercial corridor. This is the largest project in this permit batch and signals continued business activity in the area.

items.headline: City commissions outline priorities for coming year
items.summary: Saratoga City Council reviewed and approved work plans from five commissions covering heritage preservation, library and public art, parks and recreation, traffic safety, and planning. The priorities set by these commissions will guide city projects and policy focus through June 2027.

items.headline: Cupertino launches business license amnesty program for shopping centers
items.summary: The city is reaching out to shopping centers with a business license amnesty program to help businesses get into compliance. This could affect local retailers and small businesses operating in Cupertino's commercial areas.

items.headline: City announces economic incentives for retail and startup businesses
items.summary: Cupertino is introducing new economic development incentives targeting retail stores, small businesses, and startups. The city is also planning a Summer of Celebrations shop local campaign and seeking corporate sponsorships for city events.

items.headline: Great American Litter Pick-up becoming annual city-sponsored event
items.summary: The City Council approved the Great American Litter Pick-up as an official annual city-sponsored special event, authorizing city funds and donations for cleanup efforts throughout San José.

items.headline: City Announces Community Development Grant Funding Applicants
items.summary: The Housing and Human Services Commission will review applicants for HOME and Community Development Block Grant (CDBG) funding on March 25. These federal grants support affordable housing and community development projects in Sunnyvale.

items.headline: San Antonio Road Area Plan to study land use and transportation changes
items.summary: The Planning Commission is beginning a study session on alternatives for the San Antonio Road area, examining potential changes to how land is used and how people move through that part of Palo Alto. This could affect zoning, development, and traffic patterns in that corridor.

items.headline: New retail vitality policies could reshape Palo Alto's shopping districts
items.summary: The Commission is considering an ordinance to amend zoning and building rules to implement retail vitality policies from the city's Comprehensive Plan. These changes could affect how retail spaces are regulated, designed, and developed across Palo Alto's commercial areas.
